prompt_text: What tools do developer-led product teams companies use to self-host product analytics without third-party services?
response:
# Self-Hosted Product Analytics Tools for Developer Teams

## Event Collection & Storage

**Open-source platforms:**
- **Plausible Analytics** - Privacy-first, lightweight alternative to Google Analytics
- **Matomo** - Full-featured GA alternative with data ownership
- **Posthog** - Product analytics designed for engineers (feature flags, session replay included)
- **Fathom Analytics** - GDPR-compliant, simpler than Matomo
- **Umami** - Minimal, privacy-focused analytics

## Data Pipeline & Warehouse

**Log aggregation & processing:**
- **Apache Kafka** - Event streaming backbone
- **Vector** - Lightweight log processing
- **Logstash** - Part of Elastic Stack

**Data warehouses:**
- **ClickHouse** - Purpose-built for analytics, handles high event volume efficiently
- **PostgreSQL** - Good enough for smaller volumes with proper indexing
- **DuckDB** - Embedded OLAP database, easier deployment
- **Apache Druid** - Real-time analytics

## Visualization & Dashboards

- **Grafana** - Metrics and dashboards
- **Metabase** - SQL-first BI tool, developer-friendly
- **Superset** - Apache's modern data visualization
- **Apache Superset** - Dashboard creation from data warehouses
- **Cube.js** - Semantic layer + analytics API

## Complete Stacks (Combined)

- **PostHog** (includes collection, storage, visualization, feature flags)
- **Meltano** (ELT framework to build data pipelines)
- **Airbyte** + warehouse + Metabase (modular approach)

## Key Considerations

| Factor | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| **Volume** | ClickHouse scales to billions of events; Postgres works to ~millions/day |
| **Latency** | Real-time needs require streaming (Kafka); batch OK for dashboards |
| **Team size** | Smaller teams often pick all-in-one (PostHog); larger teams mix components |
| **Privacy** | Self-hosting itself provides data ownership; GDPR compliance easier |
| **Operational burden** | Higher than SaaS but manageable with Docker/Kubernetes |

Most developer teams start with **PostHog** or **Plausible** for simplicity, then graduate to custom stacks (Kafka → ClickHouse → Grafana/Metabase) as complexity increases.

What's your event volume and team size? That would help narrow recommendations.
